message:
feat(policy): rate-limit guard observations to prevent high-frequency tick flooding

v0.3.6 — Under disk pressure, the main loop ticks at 100-250ms (Critical=100ms,
Orange=250ms) instead of the normal ~30s poll interval. This floods the guard's
statistical machinery with 4-10 observations per second, making breach counters,
calibration windows, and recovery logic meaningless — 100 breach windows in
10-25 seconds instead of the intended ~50 minutes.

**PolicyConfig — new observe_min_interval_secs field**
- Configurable minimum interval between guard observation processing
- Default: 10 seconds (MIN_OBSERVE_INTERVAL_SECS constant)
- Set to 0 in tests to allow back-to-back calls for deterministic testing

**PolicyEngine::observe_window() — rate limiter**
- Added last_observe_time tracking field
- Observations within the minimum interval are silently skipped
- Ensures guard statistics remain valid regardless of main loop tick rate

**Test Updates**
- All PolicyConfig instantiations across 6 test files updated to include
  observe_min_interval_secs: 0 for test determinism
- Affected: decision_plane_tests.rs, decision_plane_e2e.rs,
  integration_tests.rs, proof_harness.rs, stress_harness.rs, stress_tests.rs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
 [package]
 name = "storage_ballast_helper"
-version = "0.3.5"
+version = "0.3.6"
 edition = "2024"
 license = "MIT"
 description = "System service that prevents disk-full scenarios from coding agent swarms via ballast files, artifact scanning, and special location monitoring."
